message:
feat(membrane): add optimal experiment design controller for probe budget allocation

New runtime math monitor: design.rs (362 lines) implementing optimal
experiment design (OED) for validation probe scheduling, grounded in
Round R18 (Bootstrap/Init/Observability Spine: "optimal experiment
design for low-overhead runtime probes with maximal fault
identifiability", math stack item #28).

OptimalDesignController maintains a small probe catalog (8 probes:
fingerprint check, canary verify, bloom precheck, TLS cache audit,
arena full scan, page bitmap walk, quarantine depth census, cohomology
sharding consistency) with per-probe cost estimates in nanoseconds and
identifiability scores (Fisher information proxy).

Core algorithm:
- D-optimal design criterion: maximize log-det of the information matrix
  (sum of selected probe outer products) subject to a nanosecond budget
  constraint and maximum probe count.
- Greedy selection: iteratively pick the probe that maximally increases
  the determinant, stopping when budget or cardinality limit is reached.
- Budget is mode-sensitive: strict mode gets 50ns probe budget (minimal
  overhead), hardened mode gets 200ns (richer diagnostics).
- identifiability_score_ppm returned for snapshot reporting (scaled to
  parts-per-million for consistency with other risk metrics).

RuntimeMathKernel integration:
- design field added to kernel struct behind Mutex.
- observe() calls design.plan(budget_ns) every 64 decisions (sampled
  to amortize the greedy selection cost).
- Snapshot extended with design_identifiability_ppm, design_selected_probes,
  design_budget_ns, design_expected_cost_ns.
- Design score does NOT contribute to risk_upper_bound_ppm (it is an
  observability optimizer, not a risk signal). It is reported for
  offline analysis of probe efficiency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
